UPC-GREENCHIPS-EDU Scholarship
METU Internships Offer
item.description:
Within the framework of the SUSA project, two partners—METU and GNOMON Informatics SA —will offer internships to degree program students. Below is a consolidated explanation of the criteria, procedures, and financial mechanisms that will govern the provision of third-party financial support related to these internships.

For METU, the maximum amount of financial support for each third-party recipient is set at €610 per internship, in accordance with internal standards for internship remuneration and as aligned with the granting authority’s guidelines.

This call (as well as the financial support that will be provided through it) is NOT an EU action. This call is launched solely by MIDDLE EAST TECHNICAL UNIVERSITY (METU). The financial support to the selected successful applicants will be provided directly by METU in its own name and under its own responsibility. METU’s own responsibility includes the preparation and drafting of this call as well as the selection of successful applicants that will receive the said financial support. The publication of this call in the EU Funding & Tenders Portal is made solely for information purposes with the aim to increase the visibility of this call to the general public, including to potential applicants (i.e. students). The said publication shall not be construed as direct or indirect approval of the content and conditions of this call by the relevant EU authorities or as any form of waiver from the above-described responsibility of METU. 

METU’s financial support amount is derived from internal remuneration benchmarks for student internships. 

For both METU and GNOMON, internships must be aligned with the thematic focus of the SUSA project, including but not limited to: 

  1. Digital health and healthcare innovation 
  2. Health data management and interoperability 
  3. Artificial intelligence applications in healthcare 
  4. Advanced digital skills development and practical application of SUSA educational content 

Internship positions will be open to students enrolled in degree programs, with a focus on engineering and health-related disciplines that are relevant to the SUSA learning objectives. 

To align with academic cycles and optimize visibility, seven internship calls are planned throughout the project. Announcement will be published in June 2025 before the start of the Fall 2025-2026 academic semester with the following cutoff dates: 

  1. September 2025 
  2. January 2026 
  3. September 2026 
  4. January 2027 
  5. September 2027 
  6. January 2028 
  7. September 2028

item.title: Gnomon Internships Offer
item.description:
Within the framework of the SUSA project, two partners—METU and GNOMON Informatics SA —will offer internships to degree program students. Below is a consolidated explanation of the criteria, procedures, and financial mechanisms that will govern the provision of third-party financial support related to these internships.

For GNOMON, the maximum amount of financial support is set at €1,500 per month of internship, calculated based on an estimated structure of 20 internships with a 4-month duration each.

  1. In cases where internships are implemented with a shorter duration than four months, the remaining budget may be reallocated to fund additional internship placements, until the total allocated amount has been fully utilized.

Basis for Financial Calculation

  1. GNOMON’s amount is based on a combination of the company’s standard cost model for internships and the actual licensing cost of the software platform used in SUSA’s shared learning experiences.

For both METU and GNOMON, internships must be aligned with the thematic focus of the SUSA project, including but not limited to: 

  1. Digital health and healthcare innovation 
  2. Health data management and interoperability 
  3. Artificial intelligence applications in healthcare 
  4. Advanced digital skills development and practical application of SUSA educational content 

Eligible Participants 

Internship positions will be open to students enrolled in degree programs, with a focus on engineering and health-related disciplines that are relevant to the SUSA learning objectives. 

This call (as well as the financial support that will be provided through it) is NOT an EU action. This call is launched solely by Gnomon Informatics SA (Gnomon) . The financial support to the selected successful applicants will be provided directly by Gnomon in its own name and under its own responsibility. Gnomon’s own responsibility includes the preparation and drafting of this call as well as the selection of successful applicants that will receive the said financial support. The publication of this call in the EU Funding & Tenders Portal is made solely for information purposes with the aim to increase the visibility of this call to the general public, including to potential applicants (i.e. students). The said publication shall not be construed as direct or indirect approval of the content and conditions of this call by the relevant EU authorities or as any form of waiver from the above-described responsibility of Gnomon. 


To align with academic cycles and optimize visibility, seven internship calls are planned throughout the project. Announcement will be published on June 2025 before the start of the Fall 2025 - 2026 academic semester with the following cutoff dates:
